'''In Search of Knowledge''' is a [[miniquest]] which involves the player filling three tomes found in the [[Forthos Dungeon]]'s Temple Library.

==Details==
{{Quest details
|start = [[Brother Aimeri]], towards the south-eastern end of the [[Forthos Dungeon]].
|startmap = 1840,9926
|difficulty = Experienced
|description = Assist [[Brother Aimeri]] in uncovering the mysteries that lie in the [[Forthos Dungeon]].
|length = Medium
|requirements = None - but a high [[combat level]] is recommended.
|items = * [[Combat equipment]]
* 5 pieces of food (must be meat, fish or vegetables)
* A [[slash weapon]] or [[knife]] to cut through webs
|recommended = * {{SCP|Combat|45|link=yes}}
|kills = Varies
|leagueRegion = {{LeagueRegion|Kourend}} - location requirement
}}
'''Note:''' This miniquest is best completed while completing [[Slayer task/Spiders|spiders]], [[Slayer task/Zombies|zombies]], [[Slayer task/Red dragons|red dragons]], or [[Sarachnis|Sarachnis]] Slayer tasks, which effectively turns this miniquest into a passive grind, which saves time in the long run.

==Walkthrough==
Enter [[Forthos Dungeon]] and talk to the injured monk, [[Brother Aimeri]], located in the south-east corner of the dungeon, outside the entrance to [[Sarachnis]]' lair. Use five pieces of food (must be fish, meat or vegetables), and he will heal to full health. Ask him who he is, and he will say he is here to discover the origins of his religion, and asks that you let him know about anything of interest that you find. Once you say "I'll keep my eyes open", the miniquest will begin. 

The player must now gather four [[tattered sun page]]s, [[tattered moon page]]s, and [[tattered temple page]]s. The pages are dropped by all types of monsters within the Dungeon at the rates shown below.

* At lower levels, [[Baby red dragon|baby red dragons]] are the easiest to kill for the pages - there is an alcove in the south-east corner of the [[Forthos Dungeon#Scorched Grotto|Scorched Grotto]] where two baby red dragons spawn and the adult dragons cannot reach.
* [[Temple Spider|Temple spiders]] are quicker to kill, due to having more spawns and being in a [[multicombat area]]. While the [[Dwarf multicannon|cannon]] is not available for use in the dungeon, it is extremely efficient to use [[Ancient Magicks|ancient spells]], [[Chinchompa|chinchompas]], or other multi-target attacks on the spiders to find all pages quickly.
* The [[Undead Druid|undead druids]] in the south-west room offer players a less click-intensive and more sustainable method to hunt for the pages. Players can avoid all damage by attacking them from outside of melee range and praying against magic, restoring their prayer points at the nearby [[Altar (Forthos Dungeon)|altar]] when needed. Players can kill the druids indefinitely until the pages are found without needing to resupply. Additionally, the druids are [[undead]] and thus susceptible to the effects of the [[Salve amulet(ei)|salve amulet]]. '''Note: This method does not work if [[Olbertus]] has [[Cursed Altar|cursed the altar]].'''

Once four of each page has been obtained, head west from Brother Aimeri and then north from the [[Grubby Chest|grubby chest]] into a library area with [[undead druid]]s; it is recommended to have the [[Protect from Magic]] [[Prayer]] active, as their magic attacks are accurate and hit hard. Search all except the north-western [[musty bookshelf|bookshelves]] in the temple library; you should have obtained three tomes: the [[tome of the sun]], [[tome of the moon]], and [[tome of the temple]].
[[File:Forthos Dungeon - Temple Library.png|thumb|The temple library, where the tomes are found.]]
Use the pages on the respective tomes. Once filled, they can be used on [[Logosia]] in the centre of the [[Arceuus Library]], completing the miniquest.

==Reward==

* A [[lamp of knowledge]] that gives 10,000 experience in a skill of your choice that is at least level 40.

Upon completion, additional pages received can be sold to Logosia in exchange for 1,000 coins per page. You may also tell Logosia, ''"I'm going to stop looking for temple, sun and moon pages"'' to stop receiving them.

==Transcript==
{{hastranscript|Miniquest}}

==Trivia==
*After completing the miniquest, as well as having used the [[temple coin]] on the [[Stone Relief]], the player will be able to inform the [[Knight of Varlamore (Kourend Castle)|Knight of Varlamore]] about the [[Forthos Dungeon]]. Upon doing so, he will travel to the Altar of the Sun within the dungeon, as he claims the temple may be of help in his mission.
*The player character responds to Brother Aimeri saying "the greater good" by repeating "the greater good." This is a reference to the 2007 British comedy film ''Hot Fuzz'' in which characters repeat the phrase "the greater good" after one another.
